Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Intermediate A targeted ransomware attack on a healthcare organization begins with spear-phishing to gain initial access, escalates privileges via exposed medical devices, and encrypts critical patient data for financial extortion.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Social engineering", "Vulnerability", "Internet-facing assets", "Shared DB or directory", "Obsolete products"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-1380 [CWE-250]: Improper Restriction of Reauthentication After Initial Authentication
stage.description: Attackers sent spoofed emails impersonating health authorities containing malicious Excel attachments with embedded macros. Recipients opening these files triggered macro execution, establishing initial foothold via CVE-2020-1380.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2018-4344 [CWE-250]: Improper Restriction of Reauthentication After Initial Authentication
stage.description: Macro payloads exploited unpatched Microsoft Office instances to deploy Cobalt Strike beacons, leveraging known vulnerabilities in legacy healthcare applications to execute arbitrary code.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A hidden scheduled task was created in the Windows Task Scheduler to automatically restart the beacon payload after system reboots, maintaining persistent access to the network.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Default credentials were used to access exposed RDP ports on medical imaging devices (PACS systems), allowing privilege escalation to administrative accounts.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 636: Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: C2 traffic was encrypted with AES-128 and disguised as legitimate telehealth traffic to evade detection by network monitoring tools.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Ransomware encrypted patient records and billing databases, appending .locked extensions to files and displaying a ransom note demanding Bitcoin payments for decryption keys.

Scenario.summary: Campaign C0027 exploited human and technical vulnerabilities in a healthcare provider's environment through phishing, lateral movement, and ransomware deployment, resulting in operational disruption and financial extortion demands.
